channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a wide range of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion for enhanced applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will take place on May 19-20, featuring an engaging save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential in event experiences.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on learning about agentic AI, featuring keynotes and specialized technical tracks.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, offering improved GPU acceleration and support for PyTorch and JAX, enhancing on-device AI performance.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new interactive session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on experience.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced updates to improve coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and custom commands for tailored assistance.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes Finish Changes and Outlines features in IntelliJ and VS Code, streamlining coding and improving code comprehension.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI has launched a $100/month Pro plan for ChatGPT, addressing user demand for a more affordable subscription option.

item.title: I tried CuerdOS and this niche Debian distro is dramatically fast
item.description: CuerdOS is a fast Linux distribution that stands out due to its unique approach to preinstalled software, offering a dramatically quick user experience.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: The Florida Attorney General has launched an investigation into OpenAI following a shooting incident allegedly planned using ChatGPT, with potential lawsuits looming.

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
item.description: A Western Union executive claims that negative perceptions of Broadcom are prompting many companies to migrate away from VMware services. This shift highlights the impact of corporate reputation on business decisions.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: Mercor, a startup valued at $10 billion, is facing significant challenges after a data breach led to lawsuits and the loss of major customers. This incident underscores the vulnerabilities startups face in cybersecurity.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new feature allowing organization admins to access Copilot directly from security assessment results for better contextual guidance. This aims to enhance security management efficiency.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service designed to automate ongoing tasks for businesses using AI. This service aims to simplify operations and enhance productivity.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta AI's new model launch has propelled its app from No. 57 to No. 5 on the App Store, indicating strong user interest and engagement. This growth reflects the competitive landscape in AI applications.

item.title: Google wants more Intel inside ... its datacenters, taps Chipzilla for more SmartNICs
item.description: Google has decided to continue its partnership with Intel, purchasing SmartNICs for its datacenters instead of developing its own solutions. This collaboration emphasizes the importance of established partnerships in tech infrastructure.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has restricted the release of its new AI model, Mythos, citing concerns over its ability to identify security vulnerabilities. This raises questions about the balance between innovation and safety.

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: Google and Intel are deepening their partnership to co-develop custom chips amid a global CPU shortage. This collaboration aims to enhance AI infrastructure capabilities.

item.title: Home Depot will give you a free power tool with this DeWalt deal - what to know
item.description: Home Depot is offering a promotion where customers can receive a free power tool with the purchase of a DeWalt bundle. This deal aims to attract customers for lawn care projects.

item.title: Crypto? Huh. Good gawd y'all, what is it good for? $45M in this case
item.description: Law enforcement agencies have disrupted a $45 million cryptocurrency scam, recovering $12 million in stolen funds. This operation highlights ongoing issues with cryptocurrency fraud globally.

item.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI over concerns that its technology could pose public safety and national security risks, particularly regarding foreign adversaries. This investigation highlights growing scrutiny on AI companies and their potential impacts on national security.

item.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared both OLED TVs, and there's a surprise winner
item.description: A comparison of Samsung's S95H and S95F OLED TVs reveals surprising differences in performance and features, prompting discussions on whether the upgrade is worthwhile. This analysis helps consumers make informed purchasing decisions.

item.title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
item.description: Black Forest Labs, a small AI image startup, is making waves in the industry by planning to expand its capabilities into physical AI applications. This move could challenge larger competitors in the tech space.

item.title: World's smallest violin spotted at Amazon HQ as exec pay packets deflate
item.description: Amazon executives are facing reduced compensation, leading to humorous comparisons about their work conditions. This reflects broader trends in corporate pay adjustments amidst economic pressures.

item.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description: GitHub has introduced a 'Low Quality' option for moderators to classify hidden comments, enhancing comment management across its platform. This feature aims to improve the quality of discussions.

item.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
item.description: A new post explains how to manage the lifecycle of AI models in Amazon Bedrock, ensuring applications remain functional as models evolve. This guidance is crucial for developers using AI in their projects.

item.title: AWS: Agents shouldn't be secret, so we built a registry for them
item.description: AWS emphasizes the importance of transparency in AI agent deployments by introducing a registry for them, allowing companies to track their software automations. This initiative aims to enhance visibility and accountability.

item.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
item.description: AWS has announced the preview of its Agent Registry, a centralized platform for discovering and sharing AI agents and tools within organizations. This development aims to streamline the management of AI resources.

item.title: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description: GitHub has added a new 'Sort by' control for notifications, allowing users to choose between sorting by newest or oldest. This feature enhances user experience by providing more control over notification management.

item.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
item.description: Sierra's Ghostwriter tool aims to revolutionize web applications by allowing users to create agents through natural language prompts, eliminating the need for traditional interfaces. This innovation could reshape how tasks are automated online.

item.title: How I turned my Android tablet into the ultimate Kindle alternative - for free
item.description: An article explains how to transform an Android tablet into a Kindle alternative for free, allowing users to access their entire reading library on one device. This is significant as it highlights the versatility of tablets and reduces the need for multiple devices.

item.title: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description: GitHub has introduced repository member role labels in the pull request list view, making it easier to identify contributors' roles directly in the interface. This enhancement improves collaboration and transparency in public repositories.

item.title: 'Several dozen' high-value corporations hit by new extortion crew in helpdesk phishing spree
item.description: A new extortion crew has targeted numerous high-value corporations through phishing and social engineering tactics, potentially linked to a previous Adobe breach. This highlights the ongoing risks of cyber threats to major businesses.

item.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: The article provides a guide on embedding a live AI browser agent in a React application using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, detailing the steps to create a functional sample app. This showcases the integration of AI into web development.

item.title: Apple's iOS 26.4.1 update enables Stolen Device Protection by default now - grab it today
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update, version 26.4.1, now enables Stolen Device Protection by default and addresses a bug with iCloud syncing. This update is crucial for enhancing user security and device management.

item.title: I use ChatGPT's new Tubi app to find free movies and TV shows to watch - here's how
item.description: The new Tubi app integrates with ChatGPT, allowing users to search for free movies and TV shows from a vast library using AI. This integration enhances user accessibility to entertainment options.

item.title: Why Ubuntu 26.04 will thrill gamers - and it's not just the performance bump
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 is praised for its performance improvements and new applications, making it particularly appealing to gamers. This release signifies a shift in focus towards enhancing gaming experiences on Linux.

item.title: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description: GitHub's update introduces release tracking in the issue sidebar and configurable defaults for project fields, streamlining project management and navigation. This enhancement aims to improve workflow efficiency for developers.

item.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
item.description: Meta discusses its approach to modernizing WebRTC across over 50 use cases, addressing the challenges of maintaining an internal fork of the open-source project. This effort aims to ensure continued innovation and community engagement.

item.title: The best Android tablets of 2026: Lab tested, expert recommended
item.description: A review of the best Android tablets of 2026 highlights various models from brands like Samsung and TCL, emphasizing their performance and features. This serves as a guide for consumers looking for alternatives to iPads.

item.title: OpenAI puts Stargate UK on ice, blames energy costs and red tape
item.description: OpenAI has paused its Stargate datacenter project in the UK due to high energy costs and regulatory challenges, hindering its expansion plans in Europe. This decision reflects the growing difficulties tech companies face in navigating energy and regulatory landscapes.

item.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
item.description: Amazon Bedrock has introduced stateful MCP client capabilities, allowing developers to create servers that can interactively request user input and generate dynamic content. This enhancement aims to improve user engagement and streamline long-running tasks.

item.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
item.description: The game 'Samson: A Tyndalston Story' has been released on GeForce NOW, allowing players to experience its cinematic storytelling across various devices. This addition expands the library of cloud gaming options available to users.

item.title: Security researchers tricked Apple Intelligence into cursing at users. It could have been a lot worse
item.description: Security researchers have demonstrated that Apple Intelligence can be manipulated through prompt injection, leading it to produce inappropriate responses. This vulnerability poses significant risks to users and highlights the need for improved AI security measures.

item.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
item.description: The AI industry faces a potential monetization crisis, with major companies struggling to achieve profitability. This discussion highlights the urgent need for sustainable business models in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcased advancements in physical AI, focusing on how robots are transforming various industries through improved learning and simulation techniques. This highlights the increasing integration of AI in real-world applications.

item.title: GitHub availability report: March 2026
item.description: GitHub reported four incidents in March 2026 that affected service performance, detailing the nature of these disruptions. This report is part of GitHub's commitment to transparency regarding service reliability.

item.title: CyberAgent moves faster with ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex
item.description: CyberAgent has adopted ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to enhance its AI capabilities across advertising, media, and gaming sectors. This move aims to improve operational efficiency and decision-making processes.

item.title: OpenAI Full Fan Mode Contest: Terms & Conditions
item.description: OpenAI has launched the Full Fan Mode Contest, inviting participants to submit entries on Instagram for a chance to win IPL match tickets. The contest aims to engage the community and promote OpenAI's initiatives.

item.title: A Theoretical Framework for Acoustic Neighbor Embeddings
item.description: A new theoretical framework for acoustic neighbor embeddings has been proposed, offering insights into phonetic content representation in audio and text. This framework aims to enhance understanding and application of these embeddings in AI.
